what_it_does: This file is a draft noticeboard item that records the outcome of research and the intended architecture for adding live chat over channels, scoped to a fork.
observations: Draft noticeboard item with id item-90a639e7 in the dragnet-development channel
Title frames the work as research and architecture for a fork
Body claims 'verified research' on a channel-injection mechanism and a clean architecture
Lists four architectural elements: register app as pushable member, symmetric push, SSE to browser, identity blocker, and build order
Includes an ordered list of six related board items
Created and updated within the same minute by session ch-3mpkjg3r
No code symbols or implementation details are present
standouts: The app is to be registered as a pushable member of a channel
Delivery to the browser uses Server-Sent Events (SSE)
An unspecified identity blocker is treated as a first-class architectural constraint
The work is explicitly scoped to a fork
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item frontmatter schema with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, order, created, updated, and history
Uses a short markdown body as a status/research note
References related items by board:// URI in an order array
concerns: The body asserts 'verified research' without citations or evidence in the file
Content is terse and lacks concrete architecture details
The identity blocker and build order are named but not explained
All related items are referenced but not present here
notes: A thin research-and-architecture noticeboard entry that anchors a fork's live-chat design around app-as-pushable-member, symmetric push, SSE delivery, and an identity blocker.
questions: What exactly is the channel-injection mechanism?
What does 'register the app as a pushable member' mean in this codebase?
What is the identity blocker and how does it operate?
Which codebase or fork is this scoped to?
What do the six ordered related items cover?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches the content direction, but the actual file is a terse summary rather than detailed research or architecture documentation.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to establish and communicate a compact architectural foundation for live chat in a fork, centering on a symmetric push pattern where the app participates as a pushable channel member and the browser receives events via SSE, while an identity blocker gates participation.
novelty: The framing of the application itself as a pushable member of a channel, combined with symmetric push and SSE browser delivery, is a distinctive way to model chat transport.
contribution: A concise anchor that states the intended live-chat architecture for a fork, naming the key design choices and providing an ordered set of related noticeboard items for further context.
summary_for_embedding: Draft noticeboard item item-90a639e7 records research and architecture for live chat over channels in a fork, citing a channel-injection mechanism, app-as-pushable-member registration, symmetric push, SSE-to-browser delivery, an identity blocker, and a build order.
intention_for_embedding: This file was meant to capture and communicate the verified architectural direction for adding live chat over channels in a forked codebase.
